Corrosive Bubbles | Object 40
 {{User:WindXD/Hr|color=#FFFFFF|width=80%|thickness=10px}} {| class="sortable fandom-table" |+Location: Any dangerous aquatic levels, and rarely neutral ones. (i.e, [[Level 7.7]]) |'''Class''' |Threatening |'''Corrosive Bubbles''' is infamous for its dangerous properties of organ failure, pain on contact, and intelligence properties. |- |'''Use''' |Little |'''Corrosive Bubbles''' can harm both wanderers and entities; though, harming an entity with it is hard and risky, but doable. |- |'''Value''' |None |Shown its deadly properties. |- |'''Rarity''' |Variable |'''Corrosive Bubbles''', as mentioned beforehand, can be found in dangerous aquatic levels; more specifically: isolated bodies of liquid like bottles, small ponds, or damp areas. |} {{Blurimage|file=Soap Acid.jpg|caption=A popular image of Corrosive Bubbles.|align=center|width=500px}} ==
Description
== '''Corrosive Bubbles''' are groups of reflective monochromatic bubblesRanging a few centimetres to meters. inhabiting dangerous and sometimes natural aquatic levels, specifically small isolated bodies of water of any other liquidsE.g., ponds, bottles, and damp spots.. '''Corrosive Bubbles''' are notable for their dangerous properties, causing inflammation, a stinging pain, or tissue death upon touch; organ death if consumed or inserted into one's blood, and various amounts of auditory hallucinations or effects it can cause when it is in proximity to a wanderer. '''Corrosive Bubbles''' form naturally, commonly in moderately bacteria-affected water, water or [[Object 1|Almond Water]] that has been still for a prolonged amount of time, briefly in [ DATA LOST ]. But in common cases, it can spontaneously start forming quickly into big groups of '''Corrosive Bubbles.''' These huge groups have shown a low level of predatory intelligence and stealth, able to identify the closest wanderer within a 100-meter radius and to in some cases, stop making their natural noises to attack a wanderer silently. {{Blurimage|file=Soap Alt.png|caption=An early internet image of naturally generated Corrosive Bubbles.|width=300px}} Attempts to destroy '''Corrosive Bubbles''' via forced means have not been feasible; observations have rather shown that the object can only be destroyed through natural means, such as exceeding its lifespan ~3 weeks., rare cases of spontaneous disappearances, extreme growth of microorganismsIn some bacteria cultures, 2 or 3 different types of pathogens were slightly identified. The object sample died during the test. The test, overall, only showed moderate growth of minor bacteria, which would not cause '''Corrosive Bubbles''' to die., constant movement or somehow forced into its body of water. As mentioned initially, '''Corrosive Bubbles''' has been shown to cause auditory hallucinations/effects in some close encounters. While most of the reports are inconsistent with what auditory hallucinations it causes; it can range from a single note echoing for ~5 seconds, water trickles from [[Level 37]], quiet explosions, short bursts of low pitches playing, sudden heavy reverberation or echoing, clarity shifts, transitionary pitch shifts, and in unconfirmed cases, harmonic changes, timbre shifts, or all of the mentioned auditory effects briefly occurring. The object has a numerous amount of minor properties that are listed rather then documented in one paragraph. *In huge quantities, '''Corrosive Bubbles''' can drift slowly at 30 meters per hour. *Slows down microorganism growth. *Can harm entities, generally causing lacerations, burns, and wounds. *Can dilute vital liquids and one will not feel the effects of the '''Corrosive Bubbles'''. *Has been observed to consume their dead victims in some cases. *Has been observed to change its properties ever since it was discovered. *Cannot merge with other instances. ==
Addendums
== {{LinkCollapse|num=2|initial=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/discovery.log|final=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/discovery.log|body=
Liquid Registration/Database Division Archive
''The following transcription is a log between 2 [[Backroom Colonists]] members locating and identifying a bottled instance of Corrosive Bubbles. Samples were retrieved back successfully. Recorded 2001/6/3 in an isolated part of [[Level 37]].''
START LOG
'''Explorer Stamwell''': Transmitters on. '''Explorer Case''': Stamwell, can you hear me well? '''Explorer Stamwell''': Yep. (Followed by a short pause.) '''Explorer Stamwell''': Where'd you go? '''Explorer Case''': Up a stairwell. Pretty sure I'm not that far from you. '''Explorer Case''': You? '''Explorer Stamwell''': Still on the flatland area. Can't find any stairwells. '''Explorer Stamwell''': Do you see any light up there? '''Explorer Case''': Only a few windows, but I see only [[The Void]]. '''Explorer Case''': Oh damn, wait. '''Explorer Stamwell''': Found light? '''Explorer Case''': No, but I found this weird looking bottle of [[Object 1|Almond Water]]; its like rusty... I don't know. '''Explorer Stamwell''': We have enough almond water, leave it. (Pauses for a few seconds in processing.) '''Explorer Stamwell''': Hey, can you drop that bottle? I might be able to find you. '''Explorer Case''': Sure.
A 30 second pause of silence later.
'''Explorer Stamwell''': Think I found you. '''Explorer Stamwell''': Fuck yes. '''Explorer Case''': Stop running up the stairs; you can't get injured this far in the exploration. '''Explorer Stamwell''': You don't know what the concept of excitement is? Crazy. '''Explorer Case''': Clearly you don't know what safety is, judging how you've been careless throughout the entire exploration. How were you even hired? '''Explorer Stamwell''': Okay, okay stop I was joking. '''Explorer Stamwell''': I brought up the bottle. '''Explorer Stamwell''': (A slight pause of contemplation.) I just realised there's no almond water in Level 37. Heck, not even in any of its known sublevels if I remember correctly. '''Explorer Case''': Observe what's in the bottle. Liquid, obviously. Did you forget that people can drop stuff in this level- '''Explorer Stamwell''': There's this weird foam that isn't going away. '''Explorer Stamwell''': Can we at least bring this back for analysis? '''Explorer Case''': Its fucking foam. How is that worth bringing back to base? '''Explorer Case''': Come on... we might enter [[Level 37.1]] if we don't leave this area. '''Explorer Stamwell''': Fine... fine... can you like actually see what's in here before we might skip out on a potential new object? '''Explorer Case''': Are you that delusionally ambitious...? '''Explorer Case''': 'kay then... I'll take a look for both of our sakes.
A 17 second pause of silence later.
'''Explorer Case''': Holy shit... you might be right. '''Explorer Case''': Okay...
Stamwell opens the bottle and is about to pour the soap on his hand before Case snatches it out.
'''Explorer Case''': God; do you have any idea what potentially dangerous shit is in that bottle? Like, not even a single thought about a virus? Fuck. What were you thinking? (In a slightly annoyed tone.) '''Explorer Stamwell''': Okay; we're taking it back to base still right?
END LOG
}} {{LinkCollapse|num=3|initial=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/findings.log|final=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/findings.log|body=
Liquid Registration/Database Division Archive
''Documents the initial tests the base done after Case and Stamwell brought back the object.'' '''Precautions:''' *Placed the bottle in an isolated glass chamber with a trapdoor on the top. *Filled up the bottle with clean water then dumped it into the glass chamber. We made sure that we covered the bottom of the glass with a plastic film to ensure that the object did not shatter the glass. *Tests done in the same room where it is being held for management reasons. *One of our personnel visits the room where the object is being held every 15 minutes. Tests have deemed this precaution unnecessary. '''Material test:''' *Wood: A small sample applied to the material made it waterlogged. (Update: Has not dried up after 2 days.) *Glass: No effects observed. *Rubber: The rubber became frigid and after 15 minutes, it became freeze-dried. *Plastic: No effects observed. *Paper: Hydrophobic relative; soap is now stationary after 15 minutes. '''Growth test:''' Left in a small glass chamber with a bit of water that is free from any interaction. *30 minutes: Bubbles spread out. *3 hours: Bigger bubbles observed. *5 hours: More liquid spontaneously appeared. *1 day: A lot more bubbles observed. *2 days: Glass went under thermal contraction. Glass temperature measured to be -18°C. *3 days: Effusion was observed and the test was cancelled. Note that the glass where the original sample is did not undergo the same effects listed above and only grew more bubbles. '''Attempted bacteria cultures:''' *Strain information: Unknown *Growth conditions: **Temperature: 35.3°C **Incubation period: 8 hours *Bacteria observed: **Dunaliella **Zooxanthellae **Saccharomyces cerevisiae *Additional details: **Covered 43% of the plate. **Temperature anomaly mean was -6°C. **Buzzing noise emission for brief periods during the last hour of incubation. *Observer: **U.JAKEN_PAT **U.MANDE_SAW **U.SMITH_DEDIN **Each assigned observer checks on the bacteria culture every 30 minutes.
}} {{LinkCollapse|num=4|initial=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/firstdocument.log|final=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/firstdocument.log|body=
Document is currently undergoing a review to become part of [[The Database]]. Do not modify the contents.
 {{User:WindXD/Hr|color=#000000|width=100%|thickness=2px}} Object 21, also known as "Bubble Clusters" is a recently discovered object (2001 June 3rd) that contains a multitude of currently unidentified properties, but has been emerging more frequently within aquatic levels, usually inhabiting benign bottles of Almond Water or other liquids. The characteristics of the object have been reported to take the appearance of monochromatic reflective bubbles. It thrives in small, isolated bodies of stagnant or, preferably, polluted water. The object is known to cause incessant buzzing noises akin to the fluorescent lights of [[Level 0]] when newrby. Numerous hypothesized properties include: causing burn or injuries upon contact and numerous entities exposed to it suffer critical injuries. Interestingly, the only known method of disintegration of the object is through contact with highly contaminated water, which neutralizes the object's harmful properties.

Under no circumstances should one touch, ingest, inhale, or enter proximity to the object.
}} {{LinkCollapse|num=5|initial=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/changes.log|final=+ backfile:/corrosivesoap/changes.log|body=
'''Corrosive Bubbles''' subtly changes its properties ever since its discovery. It has been shown that knowing of its previous properties causes you to forget it immediately. Additionally, '''Corrosive Bubbles''' has only changed properties 4 times, and changes are gradual. *2004 July 3rd **The object no longer causes burns but is now inflammation. **Is now restricted to dangerous aquatic levels exclusively. **Now can spawn in both small bodies of water and bottles. *2009 January 26th **Auditory effects started to be reported. **Can now grow into huge groups. **Shortened lifespan to 3 weeks rather than indefinitely (presumably). **No longer summoned liquid when it spontaneously grows. *2012 November 11th **First report of '''Corrosive Bubbles''' consuming their victims. **Now spawned in both dangerous and rarely netural aquatic levels. **Starts showing intelligence. *2019 April 7th **Formed more severe symptoms for consuming the object. **Is now able to dilute other liquids. **More natural causes can now kill '''Corrosive Bubbles'''.
}}
